channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 5 time-saving Alexa commands you’re probably not using yet
item.description: Even if you’re a regular Alexa user, there’s a good chance you haven’t discovered some of its most efficient features. Actually, strike that: There’s a good chance you’re only using your Alexa device to set timers and play music.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: Everything new on Apple TV+ in March 2025: Severance season 2 finale, Dope Thief, The Studio, and more
item.description: From the end of Severance season 2 to Mythic Quest's first spin-off show, here's what's coming to Apple TV+ in March.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Why today’s youth need more math, logic, and grammar skills
item.description: The Fast Company Impact Council is a private membership community of influential leaders, experts, executives, and entrepreneurs who share their insights with our audience. Members pay annual membership dues for access to peer learning and thought leadership opportunities, events and more.
item.dc:creator: Rodrigo Magnago, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft cancels data center lease plans, raising questions
item.description: Microsoft is slowing down or possibly redirecting its data center construction plans, according to a report by TD Cowen. Analysts with the brokerage say its a sign of the company “potentially being in an oversupply position.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How agentic AI will shape the future of business
item.description: In 2024, Amazon introduced its AI-powered HR assistant, which helps managers with performance reviews and workforce planning. Similarly, Tesla deployed AI personas to assist in real-time production monitoring and supply chain optimization.
item.dc:creator: Faisal Hoque, Fast Company

item.title: Protecting US supply chains from foreign influence
item.description: With the new Trump administration likely to place an even greater focus on reducing reliance on China, this trend is expected to accelerate. The post Protecting US supply chains from foreign influence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Top Apple exec concedes he had doubts about app store fees imposed on alternative payment options
item.description: Longtime Apple executive Phil Schiller on Monday conceded he initially opposed fees that the iPhone maker charges for in app-transactions processed outside its own payment system because he feared violating a court-ordered mandate designed to create more competition.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The rise of the TV monitor: MSI joins the likes of Samsung and LG with a smart monitor that offers Google TV and even a remote control
item.description: MSI enters smart monitor market with 27-inch Modern MD272UPSW, blending 4K UHD visuals with Google TV.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How ‘lore’ became the internet’s favorite way to overshare
item.description: Lore isn’t just for games like The Elder Scrolls or films like The Lord of the Rings—online, it has evolved into something entirely new. The Old English word made the shortlist for Oxford University Presss’ 2024 Word of the Year (though it ultimately lost to brain rot).
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk's underground digital detective reveals all to NewsNation
item.description: NewsNation's Brian Entin has the first interview with the tech genius using AI to discover alleged government waste and corruption.
item.dc:creator: Brian Entin, NewsNation

item.title: Santorini's earthquake swarm is declining gradually as thousands return to the Greek island
item.description: Scientists from the University of Athens monitoring an earthquake swarm near the island of Santorini say the phenomenon is in “gradual decline” nearly a month after the multiple tremors started.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A swarm of small drones may help artificial reefs attract sea life
item.description: A swarm of small submersibles will be used to monitor and provide data on offshore artificial reefs that are intended to attract marine life in otherwise barren sections of sea.
item.dc:creator: Menelaos Hadjicostis, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Intel targets edge, high-performance computing with extended Xeon 6 chip line
item.description: Intel has expanded its Xeon 6 line of processors, adding models 6700/6500 for high-performance cores and edge computing devices to the family. The new Xeon 6 processors feature Performance-cores (P-cores) for high performance computing.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Finding Windows 11 sluggish when you’re working with files and folders? You’re not alone – and it’s high time for Microsoft to sort out File Explorer
item.description: Some people are complaining that File Explorer is woefully slow, or that it regularly crashes, and Microsoft needs to shape up here.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: This is what no one planned could happen after taking a DNA test
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says DNA tests pose privacy risks like shared genetic information, inaccuracies and hard data deletion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Photos: The 2025 Silicon Valley Auto Show
item.description: Visitors of all ages explored the latest vehicles from 17 different manufacturers, including Toyota, Kia, Porsche, Lexus and Infiniti.
item.dc:creator: Ray Chavez, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple terminates UK data protection after government overreach
item.description: As of 10 a.m. (ET) today, Apple is no longer offering new users in the UK the chance to enable Advanced Data Protection. Essentially, anyone who now wants to enable the feature to protect their data against criminal or state surveillance or exfiltration will no longer be able to do so.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DOJ dropping immigration case against Musk's SpaceX
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) said Thursday it is planning to drop its lawsuit against Elon Musk's SpaceX, which alleged the company discriminated against refugees and asylees during the hiring process.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Zoom, Google step up their AI game with new videoconferencing features
item.description: Google and Zoom this week announced new AI features in their videoconferencing apps that the companies said are aimed at improving employee productivity. The updates focus on automating tasks during and after a videoconference.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Huge data leak exposes 14 million customer shipping records
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says Hipshipper, which is used by eBay, Shopify and Amazon sellers, exposed millions of shipping labels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Everything we know about Apple’s C1 5G modem in iPhone 16e
item.description: There’s a lot to like about Apple’s newest $599 iPhone 16e, but one of the more interesting features in the device is the Apple-designed 5G modem. Apple has spent at least seven years developing the chip, but gave it just a few short seconds of time when it announced the smartphone this week.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google’s new AI co-scientist aims to speed up the scientific discovery process
item.description: Google has unveiled an AI co-scientist built on its Gemini 2.0 platform, aiming to accelerate scientific research by generating novel hypotheses and refining experiments. While the system shows promise, questions remain about its performance and broader applicability.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
